zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre於1277年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾時，他的父親Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield11274歲，母親Clarice de Tregoz15歲。他於1294年11月19日之後與Joan Grelley在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1347年5月9日在英國英格蘭賀福爾艾亞斯 哈羅德享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾艾亞斯 哈羅德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre于1277年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾时，他的父亲Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield11274岁，母亲Clarice de Tregoz15岁。他于1294年11月19日之后与Joan Grelley在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1347年5月9日在英国英格兰賀福爾艾亞斯 哈羅德享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀福爾艾亞斯 哈羅德。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre was born in 1277, in Gloucestershire, England, his father, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, was 11274 and his mother, Clarice de Tregoz, was 15. He married Joan Grelley after 19 November 1294, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 9 May 1347, in Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre est né en 1277, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, avait 11274 ans et sa mère, Clarice de Tregoz, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Joan Grelley après 19 novembre 1294, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 9 mai 1347, à Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre 1277 in Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield 11274 und seine Mutter Clarice de Tregoz 15. Er hat nach 19. November 1294 in England, United Kingdom Joan Grelley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 9. Mai 1347 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre è nato il nel 1277 nel Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, aveva 11274 anni e il suo madre, Clarice de Tregoz, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Joan Grelley dopo il 19 novembre 1294, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 9 maggio 1347, nel Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warreが1277年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Roger la Warre Lord of Isefieldは11274歳，母Clarice de Tregozは15歳であった。1294年11月19日以後にイギリスイギリスでJoan Grelleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1347年5月9日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエウヤス ハロルドで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエウヤス ハロルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre(이)가 1277년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield(은)는 11274세였고, 그의 어머니 Clarice de Tregoz(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1294년 11월 19일 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 Joan Grelley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 5월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 와이아스 하로드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 와이아스 하로드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre nasceu em 1277, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, tinha 11274 anos e sua mãe, Clarice de Tregoz, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Grelley após 19 de novembro de 1294, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de maio de 1347, em Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre родился в 1277 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield было 11274 года, а матери, которую звали Clarice de Tregoz, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Grelley после 19 ноябрь 1294 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 9 мая 1347 г. в местности ювяс харолд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности ювяс харолд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre nació en 1277, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger la Warre Lord of Isefield, tenía 11274 años y su mamá, Clarice de Tregoz, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Joan Grelley después de 19 de noviembre de 1294, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 9 de mayo de 1347, en Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Ewyas Harold, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
